title: Atomic Withdrawal Operations
description: The system shall ensure that withdrawal operations are atomic, meaning if any part of the transaction fails, the entire operation is rolled back to maintain consistency.
rationale: To prevent partial transactions that could leave the user's account balance in an inconsistent state and avoid financial data corruption.
business_value: Maintains user trust by ensuring reliable financial transactions, reducing fraud, and preventing potential legal issues related to inconsistent account balances.

source.description: As a user, I need withdrawal operations to be atomic so that in case of failure (e.g., network outage, system crash) the transaction is fully rolled back to maintain consistency.

acceptance_criteria: Given a user has a balance of $100
When the user initiates a withdrawal of $50
And a network failure occurs during the transaction
Then the user's balance should remain $100
And the withdrawal should not be recorded
preconditions: The system is operational and connected to the necessary financial backend systems.
The user has an active account with sufficient funds.
postconditions: The user's balance remains unchanged from its initial state before the transaction attempt.
No withdrawal record should be created due to the failure of the transaction.
functional_constraints: Withdrawal operations must be executed atomically, ensuring all components of the transaction either complete successfully or fail together.
Failure in any part of the transaction process requires a full rollback to maintain data consistency.

performance: The system should handle withdrawal transactions within a predefined time frame to avoid user frustration.
availability: The system must be available 99.9% of the time to ensure reliability in critical operations.
scalability: The system should be able to scale horizontally to accommodate an increase in transaction volume during peak usage times.
usability: Error messages should be clear and user-friendly, guiding users on how to resolve issues or retry transactions.

edge_cases: Insufficient funds in user's account before initiating withdrawal
dependencies: Financial backend systems for account balance updates
Error handling and logging system to record failures

interfaces.name: Financial Database
interfaces.description: The requirement interacts with the financial database to update user balances and store transaction records.

interfaces.name: Transaction API
interfaces.description: The requirement uses an internal API to initiate and manage withdrawal transactions.

test_cases.description: User initiates withdrawal with sufficient funds; network outage during transaction processing.
test_cases.expected_result: Transaction is rolled back, user balance remains unchanged, no withdrawal record created.

test_cases.description: User initiates withdrawal with insufficient funds; system crash mid-transaction.
test_cases.expected_result: Transaction is rolled back, user balance remains unchanged, no withdrawal record created.

test_cases.description: User attempts to withdraw more than their available balance.
test_cases.expected_result: Transaction fails, user balance remains unchanged, no withdrawal record created.
